user.title: Connect to your OKOK scale
user.description: Enter (or pick) the Bluetooth MAC address of your scale.

error.invalid_mac: That doesn't look like a valid MAC address (expected e.g. F0:2C:59:F1:F0:28).

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one OKOK scale can be configured.

add_person.title: Add a person
add_person.description: This creates their sensors right away, in an "unknown" state until they've been weighed.

add_person_done.title: Step on the scale now
add_person_done.description: Have **{name}** step on the scale now - that weighing is captured as their first reference weight and reference impedance, bypassing the usual weight/impedance matching. {seconds_left} seconds left. Click **Submit** once they've stepped on to finish, or again later to check - this dialog won't close until it's captured.

remove_person.title: Remove a person
remove_person.description: Their CSV history is kept on disk; only the entities and registration are removed.

data_description.body_fat_formula: All options are BMI/age/sex based (see README) - the scale's impedance reading is logged and used to help identify who's weighing in, but not by any of the body-fat formulas themselves.

error.not_yet_weighed: No weighing detected yet. Step on the scale, then click Submit again.

abort.no_people: There are no registered people yet - add one first.
abort.registration_timed_out: {name} didn't step on the scale in time, so no reference weight/impedance was captured. They're still saved, but matching needs both to work - if anyone else in the household is already registered with a reference, {name} won't be auto-matched until you remove and re-add them to try again, or use the reassign select to manually correct their next weighing.
